title: CAT OS Language Garden plant (family), schema v1
description: One plant: a root family in the Root Grove today (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §6.1), the grouping layer the Bible asks for above vocab-NNNN word content. `garden` names which of the five gardens this plant belongs to (only 'root_grove' ships content in this first vertical slice; the other four are reserved exactly as WORD_DNA_BIBLE reserved 'confused' before that content existed). A plant never inlines a word: every member is `vocab_id` + the teaching layer authored specifically for this family (its morpheme parts, their glosses, and two context sentences — one for first meeting the family, one 'fresh' sentence for later revisits, so recognition attaches to the word, not a memorised sentence, per Bible §6.6). Exactly the taught (non held_out) members are walked in the session's Spread beat; held_out members are the Reach reserve (Bible §6.1: 'at least two reserved untaught words'), each carrying its own construct_options so the Reach beat can be scored as a construction, never a graded guess (Bible §5.5).

garden.description: The garden this plant lives in (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §4). Only root_grove ships content yet.

title.description: The root itself, e.g. "cede" — the plant's name on the Grove screen.

core_meaning.description: One or two words — the Key beat reads verbatim as '{label}. {origin_language}. {core_meaning}.' (Bible §5.1).

mentor_note.description: Optional, one quiet sentence, shown only on the plant detail page, never inside a session (Bible §7: one idea per screen).

attempt.description: The opening Encounter+Attempt (Bible §5.1): a directional read on members[0]'s teach sentence, never a definition guess.

members.description: Taught (non held_out) members are walked in Spread, 2 or 3 of them (Bible §5). held_out members are the Reach reserve, at least 2.

context_sentences.description: [0] the teaching sentence (Grow), [1] a fresh sentence reused across revisits (Bible §6.6).

construct_options.description: held_out members only: the Reach beat's construction choices, exactly one correct.
